other_observers: Mark Peterson; found earlier in the day by Doug Kibbe and Mackenzie Goldthwait and photographed by Doug.
specific_location: Two Buttes Reservoir, below the dam
description: Mark Peterson and I walked up to the pond below Two Buttes Reservoir dam, and saw a Varied Thrush fly up from the water into the trees. We saw the bird briefly, though saw the orange wing stripe on a robin sized bird. We also saw the short tail, eliminating Townsend's Solitaire. The bird was near American Robins, and was quite similar in size and shape. We looked for a quite a while and couldn't re-find the bird. Photos taken earlier in the day by Doug Kibbe are included in this report.Behaviors: Flew up from the pond into the trees, near American Robins.Call: nonePlumage: unknown
photographer: Photographer: Doug Kibbe, Doug Kibbe, Doug Kibbe, Doug Kibbe
